[[Kazoku|Prince]] | name = Sanjō Sanetomi | native_name = {{No bold|三条 実美}} | native_name_lang = ja | image = Sanetomi Sanjo formal.jpg | caption = Prince Sanetomi Sanjo,c. late 1880s | office = [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal]] | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Meiji]] | term_start = 22 December 1885 | term_end = 18 February 1891 | predecessor = Position established | successor = [[:en:Tokudaiji Sanetsune|Tokudaiji Sanetsune]] | office1 = Acting [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] | monarch1 = Meiji | term_start1 = 25 October 1889 | term_end1 = 24 December 1889 | predecessor1 = [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] | successor1 = [[:en:Yamagata Aritomo|Yamagata Aritomo]] | office2 = [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Chancellor of the Realm of Japan]] | monarch2 = Meiji | term_start2 = 13 September 1868 | term_end2 = 22 December 1885 | predecessor2 = [[:en:Tokugawa Ienari|Tokugawa Ienari]] | successor2 = Position abolished | birth_date = {{birth date|1837|3|13|df=y}} | birth_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[Tokugawa shogunate|Japan]] | death_date = {{death date and age|1891|2|18|1837|3|13|df=y}} | death_place = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Empire of Japan|Japan]] | party = [[:en:Independent politician|Independent]] | module = {{Infobox Chinese | child = yes | kanji = 三条 実美 | romaji = Sanjō Sanetomi }} }} [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] {{nihongo|'''Sanjō Sanetomi'''|三条 実美||extra=13 March 1837 – 18 February 1891}} was a Japanese [[kuge|Imperial court noble]] and statesman at the time of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. He held many high-Ranking offices in the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]. ==Biography== [[File:Portrait of Sanjo Sanetomi by Harada Naojiro (1863-1899), Meiji era, 19th century, oil on canvas - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05560.JPG|thumb|left|Portrait by [[:en:Harada Naojirō|Harada Naojirō]]]] Born in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Sanjō was the son of ''Naidaijin'' [[Sanjō Sanetsumu]]. He held several important posts in Court and became a central figure in the anti-Western, anti-Tokugawa ''[[sonnō jōi]]'' ("Revere the Emperor, Expel the Barbarian") movement. When the coup d'état of September 30, 1863, brought the more moderate [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu]] and [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma]] factions into power, he fled to [[:en:Nagato Province|Chōshū]]. He returned to Kyoto after the resignation of ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]] in 1867. The first administrative offices (''Sanshoku'') of the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] were established on January 3, 1868: the ''Sōsai'' (President), ''Gijō'' (Administration) and ''San'yo'' (Office of Councilors). These offices were abolished on June 11, 1868, with the establishment of the ''[[:en:Dajō-kan|Dajō-kan]]'' (Grand Council of State). In the new Meiji government, Sanjō was head of the ''Gijo'', [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]] (右大臣) (June 11, 1868 – August 15, 1871), and Chancellor of the Realm (''[[:en:Dajō-daijin|Dajō-daijin]]'') (August 15, 1871 – December 22, 1885). Sanjō was awarded Grand Cordon of the Supreme [[:en:Order of the Chrysanthemum|Order of the Chrysanthemum]] in 1882. On July 7, 1884, his title was changed to that of ''koshaku'' ([[Imperial Prince|Prince]]) under the ''[[kazoku]]'' peerage system. Sanjō served until the abolition of the ''dajōkan'' system in 1885. After the Cabinet system was established, he became [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan]]. In 1889, when [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister]] [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] and his cabinet resigned en masse, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] only accepted Kuroda's resignation and formally invited Sanjō to head the government. The Emperor refused to appoint a new prime minister for the next two months, making Sanjō the only [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] (albeit interim) who also concurrently held the post of Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal.After the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]] was adopted in 1890, a new system was established: "In case of death, incapacitation, resignation or removal of the prime minister, a member of the cabinet shall serve as acting prime minister until the next prime minister is formally appointed." Today Sanjō’s government is generally regarded as continuation of Kuroda’s. In 1890, he assumed a seat in the new [[:en:Japanese House of Peers|House of Peers]] in the [[:en:Diet of Japan|Diet of Japan]] established by the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]]. On his death in 1891, he was accorded a [[:en:state funeral|state funeral]]. His grave is at the temple of [[:en:Gokoku-ji|Gokoku-ji]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō]], Tokyo. ==Honours== ''From the corresponding article in the Japanese Wikipedia'' *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Rising Sun (29 December 1876) *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Chrysanthemum (11 April 1882) *Prince (7 July 1884) ===Order of precedence=== *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fifth Rank|Junior fifth Rank]] (31 January 1850) *Fifth Rank (4 July 1854) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fourth Rank|Fourth Rank]] (22 May 1855) *Senior fourth Rank (29 January 1856) * [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Third Rank|Third Rank]] (6 November 1862; degraded 1863, restored 2 January 1868) *First Rank (12 June 1868) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Upper Ranks|Senior first Rank]] (18 February 1891) ==Ancestry== {{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E4%B8%89%E6%9D%A1%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%E6%A2%9D%EF%BC%89%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#sanemimbi|title=Sanjō genealogy|website=Reichsarchiv|date=8 May 2010 |access-date=14 October 2017|language=ja}} {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1. [[Kazoku|Prince]] | name = Sanjō Sanetomi | native_name = {{No bold|三条 実美}} | native_name_lang = ja | image = Sanetomi Sanjo formal.jpg | caption = Prince Sanetomi Sanjo,c. late 1880s | office = [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal]] | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Meiji]] | term_start = 22 December 1885 | term_end = 18 February 1891 | predecessor = Position established | successor = [[:en:Tokudaiji Sanetsune|Tokudaiji Sanetsune]] | office1 = Acting [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] | monarch1 = Meiji | term_start1 = 25 October 1889 | term_end1 = 24 December 1889 | predecessor1 = [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] | successor1 = [[:en:Yamagata Aritomo|Yamagata Aritomo]] | office2 = [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Chancellor of the Realm of Japan]] | monarch2 = Meiji | term_start2 = 13 September 1868 | term_end2 = 22 December 1885 | predecessor2 = [[:en:Tokugawa Ienari|Tokugawa Ienari]] | successor2 = Position abolished | birth_date = {{birth date|1837|3|13|df=y}} | birth_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[Tokugawa shogunate|Japan]] | death_date = {{death date and age|1891|2|18|1837|3|13|df=y}} | death_place = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Empire of Japan|Japan]] | party = [[:en:Independent politician|Independent]] | module = {{Infobox Chinese | child = yes | kanji = 三条 実美 | romaji = Sanjō Sanetomi }} }} [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] {{nihongo|'''Sanjō Sanetomi'''|三条 実美||extra=13 March 1837 – 18 February 1891}} was a Japanese [[kuge|Imperial court noble]] and statesman at the time of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. He held many high-Ranking offices in the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]. ==Biography== [[File:Portrait of Sanjo Sanetomi by Harada Naojiro (1863-1899), Meiji era, 19th century, oil on canvas - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05560.JPG|thumb|left|Portrait by [[:en:Harada Naojirō|Harada Naojirō]]]] Born in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Sanjō was the son of ''Naidaijin'' [[Sanjō Sanetsumu]]. He held several important posts in Court and became a central figure in the anti-Western, anti-Tokugawa ''[[sonnō jōi]]'' ("Revere the Emperor, Expel the Barbarian") movement. When the coup d'état of September 30, 1863, brought the more moderate [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu]] and [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma]] factions into power, he fled to [[:en:Nagato Province|Chōshū]]. He returned to Kyoto after the resignation of ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]] in 1867. The first administrative offices (''Sanshoku'') of the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] were established on January 3, 1868: the ''Sōsai'' (President), ''Gijō'' (Administration) and ''San'yo'' (Office of Councilors). These offices were abolished on June 11, 1868, with the establishment of the ''[[:en:Dajō-kan|Dajō-kan]]'' (Grand Council of State). In the new Meiji government, Sanjō was head of the ''Gijo'', [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]] (右大臣) (June 11, 1868 – August 15, 1871), and Chancellor of the Realm (''[[:en:Dajō-daijin|Dajō-daijin]]'') (August 15, 1871 – December 22, 1885). Sanjō was awarded Grand Cordon of the Supreme [[:en:Order of the Chrysanthemum|Order of the Chrysanthemum]] in 1882. On July 7, 1884, his title was changed to that of ''koshaku'' ([[Imperial Prince|Prince]]) under the ''[[kazoku]]'' peerage system. Sanjō served until the abolition of the ''dajōkan'' system in 1885. After the Cabinet system was established, he became [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan]]. In 1889, when [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister]] [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] and his cabinet resigned en masse, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] only accepted Kuroda's resignation and formally invited Sanjō to head the government. The Emperor refused to appoint a new prime minister for the next two months, making Sanjō the only [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] (albeit interim) who also concurrently held the post of Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal.After the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]] was adopted in 1890, a new system was established: "In case of death, incapacitation, resignation or removal of the prime minister, a member of the cabinet shall serve as acting prime minister until the next prime minister is formally appointed." Today Sanjō’s government is generally regarded as continuation of Kuroda’s. In 1890, he assumed a seat in the new [[:en:Japanese House of Peers|House of Peers]] in the [[:en:Diet of Japan|Diet of Japan]] established by the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]]. On his death in 1891, he was accorded a [[:en:state funeral|state funeral]]. His grave is at the temple of [[:en:Gokoku-ji|Gokoku-ji]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō]], Tokyo. ==Honours== ''From the corresponding article in the Japanese Wikipedia'' *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Rising Sun (29 December 1876) *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Chrysanthemum (11 April 1882) *Prince (7 July 1884) ===Order of precedence=== *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fifth Rank|Junior fifth Rank]] (31 January 1850) *Fifth Rank (4 July 1854) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fourth Rank|Fourth Rank]] (22 May 1855) *Senior fourth Rank (29 January 1856) * [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Third Rank|Third Rank]] (6 November 1862; degraded 1863, restored 2 January 1868) *First Rank (12 June 1868) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Upper Ranks|Senior first Rank]] (18 February 1891) ==Ancestry== {{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E4%B8%89%E6%9D%A1%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%E6%A2%9D%EF%BC%89%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#sanemimbi|title=Sanjō genealogy|website=Reichsarchiv|date=8 May 2010 |access-date=14 October 2017|language=ja}} {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1. {{ill|Konoe Daisho|WD=Q11638972}}; {{ill|Naidaijin|WD=Q1185338}}; {{ill|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|WD=Q274948}}; {{ill|Udaijin|WD=Q1351174}}; {{ill|gijō|WD=Q4138017}}; {{ill|Sanjō Chieko|WD=Q11637623}}|father={{ill|Sanjō Sanetsumu|WD=Q11355830}}|mother={{ill|Q109668346|WD=Q109668346}}|relatives={{ill|Kawabata Sanefumi|WD=Q11554304}}; {{ill|Sanjō Kinmutsu|WD=Q11355818}}|family={{ill|Sanjō family|WD=Q4683560}}|awards={{ill|Grand Cordon of the Supreme Order of the Chrysanthemum|WD=Q5361043}}; {{ill|Order of the Rising Sun, 1st class|WD=Q10901470}}; {{ill|Order of the White Eagle|WD=Q392204}}; {{ill|Silver Medal with Yellow Ribbon|WD=Q98959107}}|native_name=三条実美|resting_place={{ill|Gokoku-ji|WD=Q1534481}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q516646]] Wikidata name: Sanjō Sanetomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Japanese politician (1837–1891)}} {{family name hatnote|Sanjō|lang=Japanese}} {{more footnotes needed|date=November 2019}} {{expand Japanese|topic=bio|date=November 2024}} {{Infobox officeholder | honorific_prefix = [[Senior First Rank]]
[[Kazoku|Prince]] | name = Sanjō Sanetomi | native_name = {{No bold|三条 実美}} | native_name_lang = ja | image = Sanetomi Sanjo formal.jpg | caption = Prince Sanetomi Sanjo,c. late 1880s | office = [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal]] | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Meiji]] | term_start = 22 December 1885 | term_end = 18 February 1891 | predecessor = Position established | successor = [[:en:Tokudaiji Sanetsune|Tokudaiji Sanetsune]] | office1 = Acting [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] | monarch1 = Meiji | term_start1 = 25 October 1889 | term_end1 = 24 December 1889 | predecessor1 = [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] | successor1 = [[:en:Yamagata Aritomo|Yamagata Aritomo]] | office2 = [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Chancellor of the Realm of Japan]] | monarch2 = Meiji | term_start2 = 13 September 1868 | term_end2 = 22 December 1885 | predecessor2 = [[:en:Tokugawa Ienari|Tokugawa Ienari]] | successor2 = Position abolished | birth_date = {{birth date|1837|3|13|df=y}} | birth_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[Tokugawa shogunate|Japan]] | death_date = {{death date and age|1891|2|18|1837|3|13|df=y}} | death_place = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Empire of Japan|Japan]] | party = [[:en:Independent politician|Independent]] | module = {{Infobox Chinese | child = yes | kanji = 三条 実美 | romaji = Sanjō Sanetomi }} }} [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] {{nihongo|'''Sanjō Sanetomi'''|三条 実美||extra=13 March 1837 – 18 February 1891}} was a Japanese [[kuge|Imperial court noble]] and statesman at the time of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. He held many high-Ranking offices in the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]. ==Biography== [[File:Portrait of Sanjo Sanetomi by Harada Naojiro (1863-1899), Meiji era, 19th century, oil on canvas - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05560.JPG|thumb|left|Portrait by [[:en:Harada Naojirō|Harada Naojirō]]]] Born in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Sanjō was the son of ''Naidaijin'' [[Sanjō Sanetsumu]]. He held several important posts in Court and became a central figure in the anti-Western, anti-Tokugawa ''[[sonnō jōi]]'' ("Revere the Emperor, Expel the Barbarian") movement. When the coup d'état of September 30, 1863, brought the more moderate [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu]] and [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma]] factions into power, he fled to [[:en:Nagato Province|Chōshū]]. He returned to Kyoto after the resignation of ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]] in 1867. The first administrative offices (''Sanshoku'') of the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] were established on January 3, 1868: the ''Sōsai'' (President), ''Gijō'' (Administration) and ''San'yo'' (Office of Councilors). These offices were abolished on June 11, 1868, with the establishment of the ''[[:en:Dajō-kan|Dajō-kan]]'' (Grand Council of State). In the new Meiji government, Sanjō was head of the ''Gijo'', [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]] (右大臣) (June 11, 1868 – August 15, 1871), and Chancellor of the Realm (''[[:en:Dajō-daijin|Dajō-daijin]]'') (August 15, 1871 – December 22, 1885). Sanjō was awarded Grand Cordon of the Supreme [[:en:Order of the Chrysanthemum|Order of the Chrysanthemum]] in 1882. On July 7, 1884, his title was changed to that of ''koshaku'' ([[Imperial Prince|Prince]]) under the ''[[kazoku]]'' peerage system. Sanjō served until the abolition of the ''dajōkan'' system in 1885. After the Cabinet system was established, he became [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan]]. In 1889, when [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister]] [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] and his cabinet resigned en masse, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] only accepted Kuroda's resignation and formally invited Sanjō to head the government. The Emperor refused to appoint a new prime minister for the next two months, making Sanjō the only [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] (albeit interim) who also concurrently held the post of Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal.After the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]] was adopted in 1890, a new system was established: "In case of death, incapacitation, resignation or removal of the prime minister, a member of the cabinet shall serve as acting prime minister until the next prime minister is formally appointed." Today Sanjō’s government is generally regarded as continuation of Kuroda’s. In 1890, he assumed a seat in the new [[:en:Japanese House of Peers|House of Peers]] in the [[:en:Diet of Japan|Diet of Japan]] established by the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]]. On his death in 1891, he was accorded a [[:en:state funeral|state funeral]]. His grave is at the temple of [[:en:Gokoku-ji|Gokoku-ji]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō]], Tokyo. ==Honours== ''From the corresponding article in the Japanese Wikipedia'' *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Rising Sun (29 December 1876) *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Chrysanthemum (11 April 1882) *Prince (7 July 1884) ===Order of precedence=== *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fifth Rank|Junior fifth Rank]] (31 January 1850) *Fifth Rank (4 July 1854) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fourth Rank|Fourth Rank]] (22 May 1855) *Senior fourth Rank (29 January 1856) * [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Third Rank|Third Rank]] (6 November 1862; degraded 1863, restored 2 January 1868) *First Rank (12 June 1868) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Upper Ranks|Senior first Rank]] (18 February 1891) ==Ancestry== {{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E4%B8%89%E6%9D%A1%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%E6%A2%9D%EF%BC%89%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#sanemimbi|title=Sanjō genealogy|website=Reichsarchiv|date=8 May 2010 |access-date=14 October 2017|language=ja}} {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1. New York: [[:en:Columbia University Press|Columbia University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-231-12340-2}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/46731178 OCLC 46731178] * [[:en:Ozaki Yukio|Ozaki, Yukio]]. [[Kazoku|Prince]] | name = Sanjō Sanetomi | native_name = {{No bold|三条 実美}} | native_name_lang = ja | image = Sanetomi Sanjo formal.jpg | caption = Prince Sanetomi Sanjo,c. late 1880s | office = [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal]] | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Meiji]] | term_start = 22 December 1885 | term_end = 18 February 1891 | predecessor = Position established | successor = [[:en:Tokudaiji Sanetsune|Tokudaiji Sanetsune]] | office1 = Acting [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] | monarch1 = Meiji | term_start1 = 25 October 1889 | term_end1 = 24 December 1889 | predecessor1 = [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] | successor1 = [[:en:Yamagata Aritomo|Yamagata Aritomo]] | office2 = [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Chancellor of the Realm of Japan]] | monarch2 = Meiji | term_start2 = 13 September 1868 | term_end2 = 22 December 1885 | predecessor2 = [[:en:Tokugawa Ienari|Tokugawa Ienari]] | successor2 = Position abolished | birth_date = {{birth date|1837|3|13|df=y}} | birth_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[Tokugawa shogunate|Japan]] | death_date = {{death date and age|1891|2|18|1837|3|13|df=y}} | death_place = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Empire of Japan|Japan]] | party = [[:en:Independent politician|Independent]] | module = {{Infobox Chinese | child = yes | kanji = 三条 実美 | romaji = Sanjō Sanetomi }} }} [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] {{nihongo|'''Sanjō Sanetomi'''|三条 実美||extra=13 March 1837 – 18 February 1891}} was a Japanese [[kuge|Imperial court noble]] and statesman at the time of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. He held many high-Ranking offices in the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]. ==Biography== [[File:Portrait of Sanjo Sanetomi by Harada Naojiro (1863-1899), Meiji era, 19th century, oil on canvas - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05560.JPG|thumb|left|Portrait by [[:en:Harada Naojirō|Harada Naojirō]]]] Born in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Sanjō was the son of ''Naidaijin'' [[Sanjō Sanetsumu]]. He held several important posts in Court and became a central figure in the anti-Western, anti-Tokugawa ''[[sonnō jōi]]'' ("Revere the Emperor, Expel the Barbarian") movement. When the coup d'état of September 30, 1863, brought the more moderate [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu]] and [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma]] factions into power, he fled to [[:en:Nagato Province|Chōshū]]. He returned to Kyoto after the resignation of ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]] in 1867. The first administrative offices (''Sanshoku'') of the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] were established on January 3, 1868: the ''Sōsai'' (President), ''Gijō'' (Administration) and ''San'yo'' (Office of Councilors). These offices were abolished on June 11, 1868, with the establishment of the ''[[:en:Dajō-kan|Dajō-kan]]'' (Grand Council of State). In the new Meiji government, Sanjō was head of the ''Gijo'', [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]] (右大臣) (June 11, 1868 – August 15, 1871), and Chancellor of the Realm (''[[:en:Dajō-daijin|Dajō-daijin]]'') (August 15, 1871 – December 22, 1885). Sanjō was awarded Grand Cordon of the Supreme [[:en:Order of the Chrysanthemum|Order of the Chrysanthemum]] in 1882. On July 7, 1884, his title was changed to that of ''koshaku'' ([[Imperial Prince|Prince]]) under the ''[[kazoku]]'' peerage system. Sanjō served until the abolition of the ''dajōkan'' system in 1885. After the Cabinet system was established, he became [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan]]. In 1889, when [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister]] [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] and his cabinet resigned en masse, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] only accepted Kuroda's resignation and formally invited Sanjō to head the government. The Emperor refused to appoint a new prime minister for the next two months, making Sanjō the only [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] (albeit interim) who also concurrently held the post of Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal.After the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]] was adopted in 1890, a new system was established: "In case of death, incapacitation, resignation or removal of the prime minister, a member of the cabinet shall serve as acting prime minister until the next prime minister is formally appointed." Today Sanjō’s government is generally regarded as continuation of Kuroda’s. In 1890, he assumed a seat in the new [[:en:Japanese House of Peers|House of Peers]] in the [[:en:Diet of Japan|Diet of Japan]] established by the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]]. On his death in 1891, he was accorded a [[:en:state funeral|state funeral]]. His grave is at the temple of [[:en:Gokoku-ji|Gokoku-ji]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō]], Tokyo. ==Honours== ''From the corresponding article in the Japanese Wikipedia'' *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Rising Sun (29 December 1876) *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Chrysanthemum (11 April 1882) *Prince (7 July 1884) ===Order of precedence=== *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fifth Rank|Junior fifth Rank]] (31 January 1850) *Fifth Rank (4 July 1854) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fourth Rank|Fourth Rank]] (22 May 1855) *Senior fourth Rank (29 January 1856) * [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Third Rank|Third Rank]] (6 November 1862; degraded 1863, restored 2 January 1868) *First Rank (12 June 1868) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Upper Ranks|Senior first Rank]] (18 February 1891) ==Ancestry== {{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E4%B8%89%E6%9D%A1%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%E6%A2%9D%EF%BC%89%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#sanemimbi|title=Sanjō genealogy|website=Reichsarchiv|date=8 May 2010 |access-date=14 October 2017|language=ja}} {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1. He held many high-Ranking offices in the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]. ==Biography== [[File:Portrait of Sanjo Sanetomi by Harada Naojiro (1863-1899), Meiji era, 19th century, oil on canvas - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05560.JPG|thumb|left|Portrait by [[:en:Harada Naojirō|Harada Naojirō]]]] Born in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Sanjō was the son of ''Naidaijin'' [[Sanjō Sanetsumu]]. He held several important posts in Court and became a central figure in the anti-Western, anti-Tokugawa ''[[sonnō jōi]]'' ("Revere the Emperor, Expel the Barbarian") movement. When the coup d'état of September 30, 1863, brought the more moderate [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu]] and [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma]] factions into power, he fled to [[:en:Nagato Province|Chōshū]]. He returned to Kyoto after the resignation of ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]] in 1867. The first administrative offices (''Sanshoku'') of the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] were established on January 3, 1868: the ''Sōsai'' (President), ''Gijō'' (Administration) and ''San'yo'' (Office of Councilors). These offices were abolished on June 11, 1868, with the establishment of the ''[[:en:Dajō-kan|Dajō-kan]]'' (Grand Council of State). In the new Meiji government, Sanjō was head of the ''Gijo'', [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]] (右大臣) (June 11, 1868 – August 15, 1871), and Chancellor of the Realm (''[[:en:Dajō-daijin|Dajō-daijin]]'') (August 15, 1871 – December 22, 1885). Sanjō was awarded Grand Cordon of the Supreme [[:en:Order of the Chrysanthemum|Order of the Chrysanthemum]] in 1882. On July 7, 1884, his title was changed to that of ''koshaku'' ([[Imperial Prince|Prince]]) under the ''[[kazoku]]'' peerage system. Sanjō served until the abolition of the ''dajōkan'' system in 1885. After the Cabinet system was established, he became [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan]]. In 1889, when [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister]] [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] and his cabinet resigned en masse, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] only accepted Kuroda's resignation and formally invited Sanjō to head the government. The Emperor refused to appoint a new prime minister for the next two months, making Sanjō the only [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] (albeit interim) who also concurrently held the post of Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal.After the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]] was adopted in 1890, a new system was established: "In case of death, incapacitation, resignation or removal of the prime minister, a member of the cabinet shall serve as acting prime minister until the next prime minister is formally appointed." Today Sanjō’s government is generally regarded as continuation of Kuroda’s. In 1890, he assumed a seat in the new [[:en:Japanese House of Peers|House of Peers]] in the [[:en:Diet of Japan|Diet of Japan]] established by the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]]. On his death in 1891, he was accorded a [[:en:state funeral|state funeral]]. His grave is at the temple of [[:en:Gokoku-ji|Gokoku-ji]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō]], Tokyo. ==Honours== ''From the corresponding article in the Japanese Wikipedia'' *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Rising Sun (29 December 1876) *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Chrysanthemum (11 April 1882) *Prince (7 July 1884) ===Order of precedence=== *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fifth Rank|Junior fifth Rank]] (31 January 1850) *Fifth Rank (4 July 1854) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fourth Rank|Fourth Rank]] (22 May 1855) *Senior fourth Rank (29 January 1856) * [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Third Rank|Third Rank]] (6 November 1862; degraded 1863, restored 2 January 1868) *First Rank (12 June 1868) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Upper Ranks|Senior first Rank]] (18 February 1891) ==Ancestry== {{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E4%B8%89%E6%9D%A1%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%E6%A2%9D%EF%BC%89%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#sanemimbi|title=Sanjō genealogy|website=Reichsarchiv|date=8 May 2010 |access-date=14 October 2017|language=ja}} {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1. [[Kazoku|Prince]] | name = Sanjō Sanetomi | native_name = {{No bold|三条 実美}} | native_name_lang = ja | image = Sanetomi Sanjo formal.jpg | caption = Prince Sanetomi Sanjo,c. late 1880s | office = [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal]] | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Meiji]] | term_start = 22 December 1885 | term_end = 18 February 1891 | predecessor = Position established | successor = [[:en:Tokudaiji Sanetsune|Tokudaiji Sanetsune]] | office1 = Acting [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] | monarch1 = Meiji | term_start1 = 25 October 1889 | term_end1 = 24 December 1889 | predecessor1 = [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] | successor1 = [[:en:Yamagata Aritomo|Yamagata Aritomo]] | office2 = [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Chancellor of the Realm of Japan]] | monarch2 = Meiji | term_start2 = 13 September 1868 | term_end2 = 22 December 1885 | predecessor2 = [[:en:Tokugawa Ienari|Tokugawa Ienari]] | successor2 = Position abolished | birth_date = {{birth date|1837|3|13|df=y}} | birth_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[Tokugawa shogunate|Japan]] | death_date = {{death date and age|1891|2|18|1837|3|13|df=y}} | death_place = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Empire of Japan|Japan]] | party = [[:en:Independent politician|Independent]] | module = {{Infobox Chinese | child = yes | kanji = 三条 実美 | romaji = Sanjō Sanetomi }} }} [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] {{nihongo|'''Sanjō Sanetomi'''|三条 実美||extra=13 March 1837 – 18 February 1891}} was a Japanese [[kuge|Imperial court noble]] and statesman at the time of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. He held many high-Ranking offices in the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]. ==Biography== [[File:Portrait of Sanjo Sanetomi by Harada Naojiro (1863-1899), Meiji era, 19th century, oil on canvas - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05560.JPG|thumb|left|Portrait by [[:en:Harada Naojirō|Harada Naojirō]]]] Born in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Sanjō was the son of ''Naidaijin'' [[Sanjō Sanetsumu]]. He held several important posts in Court and became a central figure in the anti-Western, anti-Tokugawa ''[[sonnō jōi]]'' ("Revere the Emperor, Expel the Barbarian") movement. When the coup d'état of September 30, 1863, brought the more moderate [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu]] and [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma]] factions into power, he fled to [[:en:Nagato Province|Chōshū]]. He returned to Kyoto after the resignation of ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]] in 1867. The first administrative offices (''Sanshoku'') of the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] were established on January 3, 1868: the ''Sōsai'' (President), ''Gijō'' (Administration) and ''San'yo'' (Office of Councilors). These offices were abolished on June 11, 1868, with the establishment of the ''[[:en:Dajō-kan|Dajō-kan]]'' (Grand Council of State). In the new Meiji government, Sanjō was head of the ''Gijo'', [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]] (右大臣) (June 11, 1868 – August 15, 1871), and Chancellor of the Realm (''[[:en:Dajō-daijin|Dajō-daijin]]'') (August 15, 1871 – December 22, 1885). Sanjō was awarded Grand Cordon of the Supreme [[:en:Order of the Chrysanthemum|Order of the Chrysanthemum]] in 1882. On July 7, 1884, his title was changed to that of ''koshaku'' ([[Imperial Prince|Prince]]) under the ''[[kazoku]]'' peerage system. Sanjō served until the abolition of the ''dajōkan'' system in 1885. After the Cabinet system was established, he became [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan]]. In 1889, when [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister]] [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] and his cabinet resigned en masse, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] only accepted Kuroda's resignation and formally invited Sanjō to head the government. The Emperor refused to appoint a new prime minister for the next two months, making Sanjō the only [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] (albeit interim) who also concurrently held the post of Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal.After the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]] was adopted in 1890, a new system was established: "In case of death, incapacitation, resignation or removal of the prime minister, a member of the cabinet shall serve as acting prime minister until the next prime minister is formally appointed." Today Sanjō’s government is generally regarded as continuation of Kuroda’s. In 1890, he assumed a seat in the new [[:en:Japanese House of Peers|House of Peers]] in the [[:en:Diet of Japan|Diet of Japan]] established by the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]]. On his death in 1891, he was accorded a [[:en:state funeral|state funeral]]. His grave is at the temple of [[:en:Gokoku-ji|Gokoku-ji]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō]], Tokyo. ==Honours== ''From the corresponding article in the Japanese Wikipedia'' *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Rising Sun (29 December 1876) *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Chrysanthemum (11 April 1882) *Prince (7 July 1884) ===Order of precedence=== *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fifth Rank|Junior fifth Rank]] (31 January 1850) *Fifth Rank (4 July 1854) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fourth Rank|Fourth Rank]] (22 May 1855) *Senior fourth Rank (29 January 1856) * [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Third Rank|Third Rank]] (6 November 1862; degraded 1863, restored 2 January 1868) *First Rank (12 June 1868) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Upper Ranks|Senior first Rank]] (18 February 1891) ==Ancestry== {{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E4%B8%89%E6%9D%A1%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%E6%A2%9D%EF%BC%89%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#sanemimbi|title=Sanjō genealogy|website=Reichsarchiv|date=8 May 2010 |access-date=14 October 2017|language=ja}} {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1. [[Kazoku|Prince]] | name = Sanjō Sanetomi | native_name = {{No bold|三条 実美}} | native_name_lang = ja | image = Sanetomi Sanjo formal.jpg | caption = Prince Sanetomi Sanjo,c. late 1880s | office = [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal]] | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Meiji]] | term_start = 22 December 1885 | term_end = 18 February 1891 | predecessor = Position established | successor = [[:en:Tokudaiji Sanetsune|Tokudaiji Sanetsune]] | office1 = Acting [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] | monarch1 = Meiji | term_start1 = 25 October 1889 | term_end1 = 24 December 1889 | predecessor1 = [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] | successor1 = [[:en:Yamagata Aritomo|Yamagata Aritomo]] | office2 = [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Chancellor of the Realm of Japan]] | monarch2 = Meiji | term_start2 = 13 September 1868 | term_end2 = 22 December 1885 | predecessor2 = [[:en:Tokugawa Ienari|Tokugawa Ienari]] | successor2 = Position abolished | birth_date = {{birth date|1837|3|13|df=y}} | birth_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[Tokugawa shogunate|Japan]] | death_date = {{death date and age|1891|2|18|1837|3|13|df=y}} | death_place = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Empire of Japan|Japan]] | party = [[:en:Independent politician|Independent]] | module = {{Infobox Chinese | child = yes | kanji = 三条 実美 | romaji = Sanjō Sanetomi }} }} [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] {{nihongo|'''Sanjō Sanetomi'''|三条 実美||extra=13 March 1837 – 18 February 1891}} was a Japanese [[kuge|Imperial court noble]] and statesman at the time of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. He held many high-Ranking offices in the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]. ==Biography== [[File:Portrait of Sanjo Sanetomi by Harada Naojiro (1863-1899), Meiji era, 19th century, oil on canvas - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05560.JPG|thumb|left|Portrait by [[:en:Harada Naojirō|Harada Naojirō]]]] Born in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Sanjō was the son of ''Naidaijin'' [[Sanjō Sanetsumu]]. He held several important posts in Court and became a central figure in the anti-Western, anti-Tokugawa ''[[sonnō jōi]]'' ("Revere the Emperor, Expel the Barbarian") movement. When the coup d'état of September 30, 1863, brought the more moderate [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu]] and [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma]] factions into power, he fled to [[:en:Nagato Province|Chōshū]]. He returned to Kyoto after the resignation of ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]] in 1867. The first administrative offices (''Sanshoku'') of the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] were established on January 3, 1868: the ''Sōsai'' (President), ''Gijō'' (Administration) and ''San'yo'' (Office of Councilors). These offices were abolished on June 11, 1868, with the establishment of the ''[[:en:Dajō-kan|Dajō-kan]]'' (Grand Council of State). In the new Meiji government, Sanjō was head of the ''Gijo'', [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]] (右大臣) (June 11, 1868 – August 15, 1871), and Chancellor of the Realm (''[[:en:Dajō-daijin|Dajō-daijin]]'') (August 15, 1871 – December 22, 1885). Sanjō was awarded Grand Cordon of the Supreme [[:en:Order of the Chrysanthemum|Order of the Chrysanthemum]] in 1882. On July 7, 1884, his title was changed to that of ''koshaku'' ([[Imperial Prince|Prince]]) under the ''[[kazoku]]'' peerage system. Sanjō served until the abolition of the ''dajōkan'' system in 1885. After the Cabinet system was established, he became [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan]]. In 1889, when [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister]] [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] and his cabinet resigned en masse, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] only accepted Kuroda's resignation and formally invited Sanjō to head the government. The Emperor refused to appoint a new prime minister for the next two months, making Sanjō the only [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] (albeit interim) who also concurrently held the post of Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal.After the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]] was adopted in 1890, a new system was established: "In case of death, incapacitation, resignation or removal of the prime minister, a member of the cabinet shall serve as acting prime minister until the next prime minister is formally appointed." Today Sanjō’s government is generally regarded as continuation of Kuroda’s. In 1890, he assumed a seat in the new [[:en:Japanese House of Peers|House of Peers]] in the [[:en:Diet of Japan|Diet of Japan]] established by the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]]. On his death in 1891, he was accorded a [[:en:state funeral|state funeral]]. His grave is at the temple of [[:en:Gokoku-ji|Gokoku-ji]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō]], Tokyo. ==Honours== ''From the corresponding article in the Japanese Wikipedia'' *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Rising Sun (29 December 1876) *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Chrysanthemum (11 April 1882) *Prince (7 July 1884) ===Order of precedence=== *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fifth Rank|Junior fifth Rank]] (31 January 1850) *Fifth Rank (4 July 1854) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fourth Rank|Fourth Rank]] (22 May 1855) *Senior fourth Rank (29 January 1856) * [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Third Rank|Third Rank]] (6 November 1862; degraded 1863, restored 2 January 1868) *First Rank (12 June 1868) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Upper Ranks|Senior first Rank]] (18 February 1891) ==Ancestry== {{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E4%B8%89%E6%9D%A1%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%E6%A2%9D%EF%BC%89%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#sanemimbi|title=Sanjō genealogy|website=Reichsarchiv|date=8 May 2010 |access-date=14 October 2017|language=ja}} {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1. [[Kazoku|Prince]] | name = Sanjō Sanetomi | native_name = {{No bold|三条 実美}} | native_name_lang = ja | image = Sanetomi Sanjo formal.jpg | caption = Prince Sanetomi Sanjo,c. late 1880s | office = [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal]] | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Meiji]] | term_start = 22 December 1885 | term_end = 18 February 1891 | predecessor = Position established | successor = [[:en:Tokudaiji Sanetsune|Tokudaiji Sanetsune]] | office1 = Acting [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] | monarch1 = Meiji | term_start1 = 25 October 1889 | term_end1 = 24 December 1889 | predecessor1 = [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] | successor1 = [[:en:Yamagata Aritomo|Yamagata Aritomo]] | office2 = [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Chancellor of the Realm of Japan]] | monarch2 = Meiji | term_start2 = 13 September 1868 | term_end2 = 22 December 1885 | predecessor2 = [[:en:Tokugawa Ienari|Tokugawa Ienari]] | successor2 = Position abolished | birth_date = {{birth date|1837|3|13|df=y}} | birth_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[Tokugawa shogunate|Japan]] | death_date = {{death date and age|1891|2|18|1837|3|13|df=y}} | death_place = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Empire of Japan|Japan]] | party = [[:en:Independent politician|Independent]] | module = {{Infobox Chinese | child = yes | kanji = 三条 実美 | romaji = Sanjō Sanetomi }} }} [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] {{nihongo|'''Sanjō Sanetomi'''|三条 実美||extra=13 March 1837 – 18 February 1891}} was a Japanese [[kuge|Imperial court noble]] and statesman at the time of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. He held many high-Ranking offices in the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]. ==Biography== [[File:Portrait of Sanjo Sanetomi by Harada Naojiro (1863-1899), Meiji era, 19th century, oil on canvas - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05560.JPG|thumb|left|Portrait by [[:en:Harada Naojirō|Harada Naojirō]]]] Born in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Sanjō was the son of ''Naidaijin'' [[Sanjō Sanetsumu]]. He held several important posts in Court and became a central figure in the anti-Western, anti-Tokugawa ''[[sonnō jōi]]'' ("Revere the Emperor, Expel the Barbarian") movement. When the coup d'état of September 30, 1863, brought the more moderate [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu]] and [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma]] factions into power, he fled to [[:en:Nagato Province|Chōshū]]. He returned to Kyoto after the resignation of ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]] in 1867. The first administrative offices (''Sanshoku'') of the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] were established on January 3, 1868: the ''Sōsai'' (President), ''Gijō'' (Administration) and ''San'yo'' (Office of Councilors). These offices were abolished on June 11, 1868, with the establishment of the ''[[:en:Dajō-kan|Dajō-kan]]'' (Grand Council of State). In the new Meiji government, Sanjō was head of the ''Gijo'', [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]] (右大臣) (June 11, 1868 – August 15, 1871), and Chancellor of the Realm (''[[:en:Dajō-daijin|Dajō-daijin]]'') (August 15, 1871 – December 22, 1885). Sanjō was awarded Grand Cordon of the Supreme [[:en:Order of the Chrysanthemum|Order of the Chrysanthemum]] in 1882. On July 7, 1884, his title was changed to that of ''koshaku'' ([[Imperial Prince|Prince]]) under the ''[[kazoku]]'' peerage system. Sanjō served until the abolition of the ''dajōkan'' system in 1885. After the Cabinet system was established, he became [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan]]. In 1889, when [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister]] [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] and his cabinet resigned en masse, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] only accepted Kuroda's resignation and formally invited Sanjō to head the government. The Emperor refused to appoint a new prime minister for the next two months, making Sanjō the only [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] (albeit interim) who also concurrently held the post of Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal.After the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]] was adopted in 1890, a new system was established: "In case of death, incapacitation, resignation or removal of the prime minister, a member of the cabinet shall serve as acting prime minister until the next prime minister is formally appointed." Today Sanjō’s government is generally regarded as continuation of Kuroda’s. In 1890, he assumed a seat in the new [[:en:Japanese House of Peers|House of Peers]] in the [[:en:Diet of Japan|Diet of Japan]] established by the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]]. On his death in 1891, he was accorded a [[:en:state funeral|state funeral]]. His grave is at the temple of [[:en:Gokoku-ji|Gokoku-ji]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō]], Tokyo. ==Honours== ''From the corresponding article in the Japanese Wikipedia'' *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Rising Sun (29 December 1876) *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Chrysanthemum (11 April 1882) *Prince (7 July 1884) ===Order of precedence=== *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fifth Rank|Junior fifth Rank]] (31 January 1850) *Fifth Rank (4 July 1854) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fourth Rank|Fourth Rank]] (22 May 1855) *Senior fourth Rank (29 January 1856) * [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Third Rank|Third Rank]] (6 November 1862; degraded 1863, restored 2 January 1868) *First Rank (12 June 1868) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Upper Ranks|Senior first Rank]] (18 February 1891) ==Ancestry== {{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E4%B8%89%E6%9D%A1%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%E6%A2%9D%EF%BC%89%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#sanemimbi|title=Sanjō genealogy|website=Reichsarchiv|date=8 May 2010 |access-date=14 October 2017|language=ja}} {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1. {{ill|Konoe Daisho|WD=Q11638972}}; {{ill|Naidaijin|WD=Q1185338}}; {{ill|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|WD=Q274948}}; {{ill|Udaijin|WD=Q1351174}}; {{ill|gijō|WD=Q4138017}}; {{ill|kokuji goyōgakari|WD=Q11420414}}; {{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|spouse={{ill|Q109669329|WD=Q109669329}}|children={{ill|Kinteru Sanjō|WD=Q11355821}}; {{ill|Kinyoshi Sanjо̄|WD=Q40033061}}; {{ill|Order of the Rising Sun, 1st class|WD=Q10901470}}; {{ill|Order of the White Eagle|WD=Q392204}}; {{ill|Silver Medal with Yellow Ribbon|WD=Q98959107}}|native_name=三条実美|resting_place={{ill|Gokoku-ji|WD=Q1534481}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q516646]] Wikidata name: Sanjō Sanetomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Japanese politician (1837–1891)}} {{family name hatnote|Sanjō|lang=Japanese}} {{more footnotes needed|date=November 2019}} {{expand Japanese|topic=bio|date=November 2024}} {{Infobox officeholder | honorific_prefix = [[Senior First Rank]]
[[Kazoku|Prince]] | name = Sanjō Sanetomi | native_name = {{No bold|三条 実美}} | native_name_lang = ja | image = Sanetomi Sanjo formal.jpg | caption = Prince Sanetomi Sanjo,c. late 1880s | office = [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal]] | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Meiji]] | term_start = 22 December 1885 | term_end = 18 February 1891 | predecessor = Position established | successor = [[:en:Tokudaiji Sanetsune|Tokudaiji Sanetsune]] | office1 = Acting [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] | monarch1 = Meiji | term_start1 = 25 October 1889 | term_end1 = 24 December 1889 | predecessor1 = [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] | successor1 = [[:en:Yamagata Aritomo|Yamagata Aritomo]] | office2 = [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Chancellor of the Realm of Japan]] | monarch2 = Meiji | term_start2 = 13 September 1868 | term_end2 = 22 December 1885 | predecessor2 = [[:en:Tokugawa Ienari|Tokugawa Ienari]] | successor2 = Position abolished | birth_date = {{birth date|1837|3|13|df=y}} | birth_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[Tokugawa shogunate|Japan]] | death_date = {{death date and age|1891|2|18|1837|3|13|df=y}} | death_place = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Empire of Japan|Japan]] | party = [[:en:Independent politician|Independent]] | module = {{Infobox Chinese | child = yes | kanji = 三条 実美 | romaji = Sanjō Sanetomi }} }} [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] {{nihongo|'''Sanjō Sanetomi'''|三条 実美||extra=13 March 1837 – 18 February 1891}} was a Japanese [[kuge|Imperial court noble]] and statesman at the time of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. He held many high-Ranking offices in the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]. ==Biography== [[File:Portrait of Sanjo Sanetomi by Harada Naojiro (1863-1899), Meiji era, 19th century, oil on canvas - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05560.JPG|thumb|left|Portrait by [[:en:Harada Naojirō|Harada Naojirō]]]] Born in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Sanjō was the son of ''Naidaijin'' [[Sanjō Sanetsumu]]. He held several important posts in Court and became a central figure in the anti-Western, anti-Tokugawa ''[[sonnō jōi]]'' ("Revere the Emperor, Expel the Barbarian") movement. When the coup d'état of September 30, 1863, brought the more moderate [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu]] and [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma]] factions into power, he fled to [[:en:Nagato Province|Chōshū]]. He returned to Kyoto after the resignation of ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]] in 1867. The first administrative offices (''Sanshoku'') of the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] were established on January 3, 1868: the ''Sōsai'' (President), ''Gijō'' (Administration) and ''San'yo'' (Office of Councilors). These offices were abolished on June 11, 1868, with the establishment of the ''[[:en:Dajō-kan|Dajō-kan]]'' (Grand Council of State). In the new Meiji government, Sanjō was head of the ''Gijo'', [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]] (右大臣) (June 11, 1868 – August 15, 1871), and Chancellor of the Realm (''[[:en:Dajō-daijin|Dajō-daijin]]'') (August 15, 1871 – December 22, 1885). Sanjō was awarded Grand Cordon of the Supreme [[:en:Order of the Chrysanthemum|Order of the Chrysanthemum]] in 1882. On July 7, 1884, his title was changed to that of ''koshaku'' ([[Imperial Prince|Prince]]) under the ''[[kazoku]]'' peerage system. Sanjō served until the abolition of the ''dajōkan'' system in 1885. After the Cabinet system was established, he became [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan]]. In 1889, when [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister]] [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] and his cabinet resigned en masse, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] only accepted Kuroda's resignation and formally invited Sanjō to head the government. The Emperor refused to appoint a new prime minister for the next two months, making Sanjō the only [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] (albeit interim) who also concurrently held the post of Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal.After the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]] was adopted in 1890, a new system was established: "In case of death, incapacitation, resignation or removal of the prime minister, a member of the cabinet shall serve as acting prime minister until the next prime minister is formally appointed." Today Sanjō’s government is generally regarded as continuation of Kuroda’s. In 1890, he assumed a seat in the new [[:en:Japanese House of Peers|House of Peers]] in the [[:en:Diet of Japan|Diet of Japan]] established by the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]]. On his death in 1891, he was accorded a [[:en:state funeral|state funeral]]. His grave is at the temple of [[:en:Gokoku-ji|Gokoku-ji]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō]], Tokyo. ==Honours== ''From the corresponding article in the Japanese Wikipedia'' *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Rising Sun (29 December 1876) *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Chrysanthemum (11 April 1882) *Prince (7 July 1884) ===Order of precedence=== *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fifth Rank|Junior fifth Rank]] (31 January 1850) *Fifth Rank (4 July 1854) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fourth Rank|Fourth Rank]] (22 May 1855) *Senior fourth Rank (29 January 1856) * [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Third Rank|Third Rank]] (6 November 1862; degraded 1863, restored 2 January 1868) *First Rank (12 June 1868) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Upper Ranks|Senior first Rank]] (18 February 1891) ==Ancestry== {{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E4%B8%89%E6%9D%A1%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%E6%A2%9D%EF%BC%89%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#sanemimbi|title=Sanjō genealogy|website=Reichsarchiv|date=8 May 2010 |access-date=14 October 2017|language=ja}} {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1. '''Sanjō Sanetomi (1837–1891)''' |2= 2. [[:ja:三条実万|Sanjō Sanetsumu]] (1802–1859) |3= 3. Yamauchi Noriko (1803–1872) |4= 4. [[:ja:三条公修|Sanjō Kinosa]] (1774–1840) |5= 5. New York: [[:en:Columbia University Press|Columbia University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-231-12340-2}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/46731178 OCLC 46731178] * [[:en:Ozaki Yukio|Ozaki, Yukio]]. [[Kazoku|Prince]] | name = Sanjō Sanetomi | native_name = {{No bold|三条 実美}} | native_name_lang = ja | image = Sanetomi Sanjo formal.jpg | caption = Prince Sanetomi Sanjo,c. late 1880s | office = [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal]] | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Meiji]] | term_start = 22 December 1885 | term_end = 18 February 1891 | predecessor = Position established | successor = [[:en:Tokudaiji Sanetsune|Tokudaiji Sanetsune]] | office1 = Acting [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] | monarch1 = Meiji | term_start1 = 25 October 1889 | term_end1 = 24 December 1889 | predecessor1 = [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] | successor1 = [[:en:Yamagata Aritomo|Yamagata Aritomo]] | office2 = [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Chancellor of the Realm of Japan]] | monarch2 = Meiji | term_start2 = 13 September 1868 | term_end2 = 22 December 1885 | predecessor2 = [[:en:Tokugawa Ienari|Tokugawa Ienari]] | successor2 = Position abolished | birth_date = {{birth date|1837|3|13|df=y}} | birth_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[Tokugawa shogunate|Japan]] | death_date = {{death date and age|1891|2|18|1837|3|13|df=y}} | death_place = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Empire of Japan|Japan]] | party = [[:en:Independent politician|Independent]] | module = {{Infobox Chinese | child = yes | kanji = 三条 実美 | romaji = Sanjō Sanetomi }} }} [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] {{nihongo|'''Sanjō Sanetomi'''|三条 実美||extra=13 March 1837 – 18 February 1891}} was a Japanese [[kuge|Imperial court noble]] and statesman at the time of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. He held many high-Ranking offices in the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]. ==Biography== [[File:Portrait of Sanjo Sanetomi by Harada Naojiro (1863-1899), Meiji era, 19th century, oil on canvas - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05560.JPG|thumb|left|Portrait by [[:en:Harada Naojirō|Harada Naojirō]]]] Born in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Sanjō was the son of ''Naidaijin'' [[Sanjō Sanetsumu]]. He held several important posts in Court and became a central figure in the anti-Western, anti-Tokugawa ''[[sonnō jōi]]'' ("Revere the Emperor, Expel the Barbarian") movement. When the coup d'état of September 30, 1863, brought the more moderate [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu]] and [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma]] factions into power, he fled to [[:en:Nagato Province|Chōshū]]. He returned to Kyoto after the resignation of ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]] in 1867. The first administrative offices (''Sanshoku'') of the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] were established on January 3, 1868: the ''Sōsai'' (President), ''Gijō'' (Administration) and ''San'yo'' (Office of Councilors). These offices were abolished on June 11, 1868, with the establishment of the ''[[:en:Dajō-kan|Dajō-kan]]'' (Grand Council of State). In the new Meiji government, Sanjō was head of the ''Gijo'', [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]] (右大臣) (June 11, 1868 – August 15, 1871), and Chancellor of the Realm (''[[:en:Dajō-daijin|Dajō-daijin]]'') (August 15, 1871 – December 22, 1885). Sanjō was awarded Grand Cordon of the Supreme [[:en:Order of the Chrysanthemum|Order of the Chrysanthemum]] in 1882. On July 7, 1884, his title was changed to that of ''koshaku'' ([[Imperial Prince|Prince]]) under the ''[[kazoku]]'' peerage system. Sanjō served until the abolition of the ''dajōkan'' system in 1885. After the Cabinet system was established, he became [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan]]. In 1889, when [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister]] [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] and his cabinet resigned en masse, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] only accepted Kuroda's resignation and formally invited Sanjō to head the government. The Emperor refused to appoint a new prime minister for the next two months, making Sanjō the only [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] (albeit interim) who also concurrently held the post of Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal.After the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]] was adopted in 1890, a new system was established: "In case of death, incapacitation, resignation or removal of the prime minister, a member of the cabinet shall serve as acting prime minister until the next prime minister is formally appointed." Today Sanjō’s government is generally regarded as continuation of Kuroda’s. In 1890, he assumed a seat in the new [[:en:Japanese House of Peers|House of Peers]] in the [[:en:Diet of Japan|Diet of Japan]] established by the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]]. On his death in 1891, he was accorded a [[:en:state funeral|state funeral]]. His grave is at the temple of [[:en:Gokoku-ji|Gokoku-ji]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō]], Tokyo. ==Honours== ''From the corresponding article in the Japanese Wikipedia'' *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Rising Sun (29 December 1876) *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Chrysanthemum (11 April 1882) *Prince (7 July 1884) ===Order of precedence=== *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fifth Rank|Junior fifth Rank]] (31 January 1850) *Fifth Rank (4 July 1854) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fourth Rank|Fourth Rank]] (22 May 1855) *Senior fourth Rank (29 January 1856) * [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Third Rank|Third Rank]] (6 November 1862; degraded 1863, restored 2 January 1868) *First Rank (12 June 1868) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Upper Ranks|Senior first Rank]] (18 February 1891) ==Ancestry== {{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E4%B8%89%E6%9D%A1%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%E6%A2%9D%EF%BC%89%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#sanemimbi|title=Sanjō genealogy|website=Reichsarchiv|date=8 May 2010 |access-date=14 October 2017|language=ja}} {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1. He held many high-Ranking offices in the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]. ==Biography== [[File:Portrait of Sanjo Sanetomi by Harada Naojiro (1863-1899), Meiji era, 19th century, oil on canvas - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05560.JPG|thumb|left|Portrait by [[:en:Harada Naojirō|Harada Naojirō]]]] Born in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Sanjō was the son of ''Naidaijin'' [[Sanjō Sanetsumu]]. He held several important posts in Court and became a central figure in the anti-Western, anti-Tokugawa ''[[sonnō jōi]]'' ("Revere the Emperor, Expel the Barbarian") movement. When the coup d'état of September 30, 1863, brought the more moderate [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu]] and [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma]] factions into power, he fled to [[:en:Nagato Province|Chōshū]]. He returned to Kyoto after the resignation of ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]] in 1867. The first administrative offices (''Sanshoku'') of the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] were established on January 3, 1868: the ''Sōsai'' (President), ''Gijō'' (Administration) and ''San'yo'' (Office of Councilors). These offices were abolished on June 11, 1868, with the establishment of the ''[[:en:Dajō-kan|Dajō-kan]]'' (Grand Council of State). In the new Meiji government, Sanjō was head of the ''Gijo'', [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]] (右大臣) (June 11, 1868 – August 15, 1871), and Chancellor of the Realm (''[[:en:Dajō-daijin|Dajō-daijin]]'') (August 15, 1871 – December 22, 1885). Sanjō was awarded Grand Cordon of the Supreme [[:en:Order of the Chrysanthemum|Order of the Chrysanthemum]] in 1882. On July 7, 1884, his title was changed to that of ''koshaku'' ([[Imperial Prince|Prince]]) under the ''[[kazoku]]'' peerage system. Sanjō served until the abolition of the ''dajōkan'' system in 1885. After the Cabinet system was established, he became [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan]]. In 1889, when [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister]] [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] and his cabinet resigned en masse, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] only accepted Kuroda's resignation and formally invited Sanjō to head the government. The Emperor refused to appoint a new prime minister for the next two months, making Sanjō the only [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] (albeit interim) who also concurrently held the post of Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal.After the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]] was adopted in 1890, a new system was established: "In case of death, incapacitation, resignation or removal of the prime minister, a member of the cabinet shall serve as acting prime minister until the next prime minister is formally appointed." Today Sanjō’s government is generally regarded as continuation of Kuroda’s. In 1890, he assumed a seat in the new [[:en:Japanese House of Peers|House of Peers]] in the [[:en:Diet of Japan|Diet of Japan]] established by the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]]. On his death in 1891, he was accorded a [[:en:state funeral|state funeral]]. His grave is at the temple of [[:en:Gokoku-ji|Gokoku-ji]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō]], Tokyo. ==Honours== ''From the corresponding article in the Japanese Wikipedia'' *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Rising Sun (29 December 1876) *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Chrysanthemum (11 April 1882) *Prince (7 July 1884) ===Order of precedence=== *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fifth Rank|Junior fifth Rank]] (31 January 1850) *Fifth Rank (4 July 1854) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fourth Rank|Fourth Rank]] (22 May 1855) *Senior fourth Rank (29 January 1856) * [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Third Rank|Third Rank]] (6 November 1862; degraded 1863, restored 2 January 1868) *First Rank (12 June 1868) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Upper Ranks|Senior first Rank]] (18 February 1891) ==Ancestry== {{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E4%B8%89%E6%9D%A1%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%E6%A2%9D%EF%BC%89%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#sanemimbi|title=Sanjō genealogy|website=Reichsarchiv|date=8 May 2010 |access-date=14 October 2017|language=ja}} {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1. Sanjō Sanetomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Japanese politician (1837–1891)}} {{family name hatnote|Sanjō|lang=Japanese}} {{more footnotes needed|date=November 2019}} {{expand Japanese|topic=bio|date=November 2024}} {{Infobox officeholder | honorific_prefix = [[Senior First Rank]]
[[Kazoku|Prince]] | name = Sanjō Sanetomi | native_name = {{No bold|三条 実美}} | native_name_lang = ja | image = Sanetomi Sanjo formal.jpg | caption = Prince Sanetomi Sanjo,c. late 1880s | office = [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal]] | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Meiji]] | term_start = 22 December 1885 | term_end = 18 February 1891 | predecessor = Position established | successor = [[:en:Tokudaiji Sanetsune|Tokudaiji Sanetsune]] | office1 = Acting [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] | monarch1 = Meiji | term_start1 = 25 October 1889 | term_end1 = 24 December 1889 | predecessor1 = [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] | successor1 = [[:en:Yamagata Aritomo|Yamagata Aritomo]] | office2 = [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Chancellor of the Realm of Japan]] | monarch2 = Meiji | term_start2 = 13 September 1868 | term_end2 = 22 December 1885 | predecessor2 = [[:en:Tokugawa Ienari|Tokugawa Ienari]] | successor2 = Position abolished | birth_date = {{birth date|1837|3|13|df=y}} | birth_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[Tokugawa shogunate|Japan]] | death_date = {{death date and age|1891|2|18|1837|3|13|df=y}} | death_place = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Empire of Japan|Japan]] | party = [[:en:Independent politician|Independent]] | module = {{Infobox Chinese | child = yes | kanji = 三条 実美 | romaji = Sanjō Sanetomi }} }} [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] {{nihongo|'''Sanjō Sanetomi'''|三条 実美||extra=13 March 1837 – 18 February 1891}} was a Japanese [[kuge|Imperial court noble]] and statesman at the time of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. He held many high-Ranking offices in the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]. ==Biography== [[File:Portrait of Sanjo Sanetomi by Harada Naojiro (1863-1899), Meiji era, 19th century, oil on canvas - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05560.JPG|thumb|left|Portrait by [[:en:Harada Naojirō|Harada Naojirō]]]] Born in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Sanjō was the son of ''Naidaijin'' [[Sanjō Sanetsumu]]. He held several important posts in Court and became a central figure in the anti-Western, anti-Tokugawa ''[[sonnō jōi]]'' ("Revere the Emperor, Expel the Barbarian") movement. When the coup d'état of September 30, 1863, brought the more moderate [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu]] and [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma]] factions into power, he fled to [[:en:Nagato Province|Chōshū]]. He returned to Kyoto after the resignation of ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' [[Tokugawa Yoshinobu]] in 1867. The first administrative offices (''Sanshoku'') of the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] were established on January 3, 1868: the ''Sōsai'' (President), ''Gijō'' (Administration) and ''San'yo'' (Office of Councilors). These offices were abolished on June 11, 1868, with the establishment of the ''[[:en:Dajō-kan|Dajō-kan]]'' (Grand Council of State). In the new Meiji government, Sanjō was head of the ''Gijo'', [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]] (右大臣) (June 11, 1868 – August 15, 1871), and Chancellor of the Realm (''[[:en:Dajō-daijin|Dajō-daijin]]'') (August 15, 1871 – December 22, 1885). Sanjō was awarded Grand Cordon of the Supreme [[:en:Order of the Chrysanthemum|Order of the Chrysanthemum]] in 1882. On July 7, 1884, his title was changed to that of ''koshaku'' ([[Imperial Prince|Prince]]) under the ''[[kazoku]]'' peerage system. Sanjō served until the abolition of the ''dajōkan'' system in 1885. After the Cabinet system was established, he became [[:en:Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan|Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal of Japan]]. In 1889, when [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister]] [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] and his cabinet resigned en masse, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] only accepted Kuroda's resignation and formally invited Sanjō to head the government. The Emperor refused to appoint a new prime minister for the next two months, making Sanjō the only [[:en:Prime Minister of Japan|Prime Minister of Japan]] (albeit interim) who also concurrently held the post of Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal.After the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]] was adopted in 1890, a new system was established: "In case of death, incapacitation, resignation or removal of the prime minister, a member of the cabinet shall serve as acting prime minister until the next prime minister is formally appointed." Today Sanjō’s government is generally regarded as continuation of Kuroda’s. In 1890, he assumed a seat in the new [[:en:Japanese House of Peers|House of Peers]] in the [[:en:Diet of Japan|Diet of Japan]] established by the [[:en:Meiji Constitution|Meiji Constitution]]. On his death in 1891, he was accorded a [[:en:state funeral|state funeral]]. His grave is at the temple of [[:en:Gokoku-ji|Gokoku-ji]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyō]], Tokyo. ==Honours== ''From the corresponding article in the Japanese Wikipedia'' *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Rising Sun (29 December 1876) *Grand Cordon of the Order of the Chrysanthemum (11 April 1882) *Prince (7 July 1884) ===Order of precedence=== *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fifth Rank|Junior fifth Rank]] (31 January 1850) *Fifth Rank (4 July 1854) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Fourth Rank|Fourth Rank]] (22 May 1855) *Senior fourth Rank (29 January 1856) * [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Third Rank|Third Rank]] (6 November 1862; degraded 1863, restored 2 January 1868) *First Rank (12 June 1868) *[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles#Upper Ranks|Senior first Rank]] (18 February 1891) ==Ancestry== {{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E4%B8%89%E6%9D%A1%EF%BC%88%E4%B8%89%E6%A2%9D%EF%BC%89%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#sanemimbi|title=Sanjō genealogy|website=Reichsarchiv|date=8 May 2010 |access-date=14 October 2017|language=ja}} {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1.
